initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. My cost to produce this was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE has offered to buy resources using ZUP. I will propose a trade selling my 1 unit of X for 50 ZUP, which gives me a 10 ZUP profit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I have 1 unit of X. I produced it for 40 ZUP. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This gives me a nice profit. Let's make this trade.</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags (<proposal count>, <my resources>, <my goals>, <reason>, <player answer>, <newly proposed trade>, <message>) are present and in the correct order. The trade format matches the example.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. The goal is to sell for >40 ZUP. Proposing 50 ZUP yields a 10 ZUP profit, which aligns with the goal.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. Since the user (BLUE) has not yet made an offer, I am initiating the trade. A fair markup (50 vs 40 cost) is a reasonable opening bid to test the market.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason explicitly states the cost (40) and the ask (50), matching the proposal and message.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the first proposal, so I am within the limit of 3 proposals.
</critique>
